    [Comment 9] JIRAUSER62328 (Tue, 27 Jan 2026 15:26:00 +0100):

    SASS compiler

    Update to angular 19, brought sass@1.85.0 which marks usages of "@import" in scss files as deprecated. As we were using it extensively build- and browser consoles are spammed with warnings.

    Was able to migrate to new syntax in stylesheets of components, however not in global stylesheets, which are importing 3rd party stylesheets we have no control over (boostrap4, bnotk/ds-ng).

        Hi everyone,

        ticket got renamed to Angular 19.

        Filip Misiorek & Oleksii Samusenko your T&M efforts for analysis please still book on this ticket. I'll update the description of above's ticket soon to fit to this task, once confirmed by [Comment 14] JIRAUSER62328 (Tue, 13 Jan 2026 14:21:58 +0100):

        ag-grid has quite extensive list of breaking changes, accommodating them will take some effort:
        https://www.ag-grid.com/javascript-data-grid/upgrading-to-ag-grid-32/#breaking-changes
         
        Should be on UVZ:
        node.js v22.12.0
        "typescript": "5.9.0", (5.8.0 with Angular 19.2)
         
        From Angular documentation  RxJS should be set to ^6.5.3 or ^7.4.0, so we are fine with that because we are now using rxjs 7.4.0 version.
         
        This part is basing on:

implementation_steps: 1. Update Node.js version in the build environment to the version required by Angular 19 (e.g., Node 20.x).
2. Upgrade TypeScript to the minimum version compatible with Angular 19 (e.g., TS 5.3).
3. Modify package.json to set @angular/core and @angular/cli to version 19, and update the Pattern Library (bnotk/ds-ng) to 12.6.0.

4. Update third‑party dependencies to versions compatible with Angular 19: ng-bootstrap (including the custom patch from UVZUSLNVV-5824), ng-select, ng-option-highlight, ag-grid-angular, and ag-grid-community.
5. Run `ng update @angular/cli @angular/core` and resolve any migration warnings.
6. Refactor imports and APIs in each affected component (e.g., ActionRestServiceImpl, JsonAuthorizationRestServiceImpl, etc.) to use Angular 19 syntax and the new Pattern Library components.
7. Verify that the vertical action bar is still functional; if deprecated warnings appear, add the required compatibility module until PL 13.2.0.
8. Remove any code, modules, or styles that are exclusive to Angular 18 or PL 11.3.1 and not required by Angular 19 / PL 12.6.0.
9. Adjust CI/CD pipeline scripts to use the new Node.js, TypeScript, and Angular CLI versions.
10. Execute linting (`ng lint`) and apply automatic fixes where possible.
11. Run the full unit test suite; update failing tests to match Angular 19 APIs.
12. Run integration tests for all discovered interfaces (e.g., GET /logger, POST /jsonauth/…) and fix any contract breaks.
13. Update documentation and release notes to reflect the new Angular and Pattern Library versions.
14. Perform a security scan to ensure CORS and CSRF configurations remain correct after the upgrade.

unit_tests: Validate that each controller component compiles and instantiates with Angular 19.
Test that services injected into ActionRestServiceImpl and other controllers behave as expected after dependency upgrades.
integration_tests: End‑to‑end verification of all REST endpoints (GET /logger, POST /jsonauth/…) against the upgraded stack.
Confirm that the vertical action bar renders correctly in UI tests up to PL 13.2.0.

security_considerations: Ensure CORS configuration allows only trusted origins (as identified in the cors pattern for ActionRestService, JsonAuthorizationRestServiceImpl, and AbstractSecurityConfiguration).
Verify CSRF protection is enabled for all state‑changing operations (csrf pattern in AbstractSecurityConfiguration).
Maintain pre_authorize checks on KeyManagerRestService (pre_authorize security pattern).
validation_strategy: Add @NotNull annotations where missing (not_null on DeedEntry).
Apply @Valid to DTOs used in DeedEntryRestService (valid pattern).
Enforce @NotBlank, @Min, and @Max constraints on KmConfig fields (not_blank, min, max patterns).
error_handling: Create or reuse custom RuntimeException classes for domain errors (custom_exception pattern).
Add @ExceptionHandler methods for generic Exception, HttpClientErrorException, and HttpRequestMethodNotSupportedException in a global exception handler.

layer_compliance: Check if changes follow layered architecture

development_plan.complexity_reasoning: The upgrade touches core framework versions and multiple third‑party libraries, requiring coordinated changes across many components, but does not introduce new architectural layers.
risks: Breaking changes due to Angular 19 deprecations may cause runtime errors.
Third‑party libraries might not have stable releases for Angular 19, leading to compatibility issues.
CI/CD pipeline failures if environment versions are not aligned.
Potential regression in UI components that rely on deprecated vertical action bar behavior.
